The term "Molotov cocktail" was coined during the Winter War between Finland and the Soviet Union (1939–40). It refers to the use of these devices by Finnish forces against Soviet tanks.The first recorded use of this weapon was in the Spanish Civil War, where it was used by both sides. In World War II, they were used extensively by resistance fighters in occupied Europe.
